content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN. Find if it is possible to represent NNN as the sum of several(possibly zero) 222's and several(possibly zero) 777's.

Formally, find if there exist two integers X,Y (X,Y≥0)X, Y \ (X, Y \ge 0)X,Y (X,Y≥0) such that 2⋅X+7⋅Y=N2 \cdot X + 7\cdot Y = N2⋅X+7⋅Y=N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line `YES` if it is possible to represent NNN as the sum of several(possibly zero) 222's and several(possibly zero) 777's and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in either uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output "YES" if N can be expressed as 2·X + 7·Y with X,Y ≥ 0, otherwise output "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All numbers are >5, thus representable. Examples: 6=2*3, 8=2*4, 9=2*1+7*1, 10=2*5, 13=2*3+7*1.

solution.approach: Since gcd(2,7)=1, every integer greater than the Frobenius number 2*7-2-7 = 5 can be represented as 2·X + 7·Y. For N ≤ 5 we check manually: N=1,3,5 are impossible, while N=2 and N=4 are possible. Therefore the answer is "NO" only for N in {1,3,5}; for all other N the answer is "YES". The algorithm simply checks this condition for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
